mentions.note: Spinning off orthopedic unit (Depuy Synthes); separation raises pharma valuation by removing slower-growth business. Stock down $5+ intraday; solid entry point on spinoff thesis.
High-quality defensive; thrives in weak economy via essential pharma/consumer products; deserves to go higher but rally pace too fast on sentiment shift, not fundamentals.

Pure-play pharma after orthopedics spinoff; talc lawsuits no longer a stock-moving factor as company fights claims individually; best drugs in pipeline shine without commoditized knee/hip business holding it back.

Strong pharma growth (15% ex-Stellara), oncology cures (83% progression-free survival multiple myeloma), $5B mental health opportunity (Capilano), solid MedTech cadence, vision care leadership; litigation risks diminishing.
Solid earner with dividends, not expensive vs. rest of market; benefiting from rotation out of speculative tech into quality names.
Consumer healthcare products; tangible, understable business; makes things people need; high demand.
February winner; terrific drug company; defensive healthcare play.
Psoriasis pill approval positive; stock down despite fundamentals due to macro pessimism—contrarian opportunity.

Icotide FDA approval masks in oil selloff; oral IL-23 antagonist for psoriasis could carve $5.5–7.5B from $22B injectable market. Medical device solid, pharma resilient, baby powder lawsuits fading. Defensive slowdown play.
Tobacco settlement case example: stock fell from $140 to $100s on litigation narrative, then recovered $140→$230 when facts cleared; Meta will follow similar pattern but timing uncertain.
Favorite pharma; most blockbusters and best pipeline; company transformed shedding slower divisions; typically hammered on news release, rallies on call; reports next week.

Healthcare's best drug stock (second to Eli Lilly) trading into free fall. Icotid (oral psoriasis pill) FDA approved; talc litigation resolved; orthopedics spinoff unlocks value. Buy on weakness — portfolio hedge vs. parabolic tech; K-shaped recovery play.
Triple-A balance sheet (one of two alongside MSFT); best blockbuster pipeline of any pharma; trading <20x earnings despite quality—undervalued in sector rotation.
Well down from highs, great balance sheet, many initiatives underway, healthcare exposure play.
Defensive alternative to volatile growth plays like Core Weave; doesn't give up gains that could blow a hole in portfolio; steadier optionality for risk-averse positioning.
Bessembinder research identified J&J as one of 17 stocks with 5 million percent cumulative returns; healthcare leader with secular growth.

10 commercialized drugs generating >$1B each; Shockwave and Cardio Repair acquisitions accelerating medtech; divesting commodity hip/knee business. No major patent cliffs near-term; buy slowly as fortress healthcare play with very little support.

Core defensive holding in the charitable trust providing cushion during today's data-center meltdown. Strong new drug pipeline, triple-A balance sheet better than U.S. Preserved sanity during tsunami sell-off.
Shedding slower divisions to focus fast-growing drugs; AAA balance sheet stronger than US government. Emerging healthcare leadership rotation.
